fitSummary.rationale: The candidate has strong adjacent foundations in networking, security concepts, operating systems, and Azure basics, which align well with entry or early mid-level cyber technician roles. The main limitation is the lack of explicit hands-on security operations evidence, tools, and measurable cyber outcomes. In the UK market, this is still a realistic transition if the profile is repositioned and supported with practical labs or certifications.

skillGaps.skill: SIEM operations and log analysis using Microsoft Sentinel or Splunk
skillGaps.learningPath: Complete the Microsoft Sentinel learning path and build a home lab that ingests Windows, Linux, and Azure logs to create 5 detection rules.

skillGaps.skill: Endpoint detection and response (EDR/XDR) tooling such as Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
skillGaps.learningPath: Use Microsoft Learn for Defender for Endpoint and simulate endpoint alerts in a lab to document triage and containment workflows.

skillGaps.skill: Vulnerability assessment and remediation with Nessus or OpenVAS
skillGaps.learningPath: Install OpenVAS or use a Nessus trial, scan a small lab network, and produce a remediation report with risk prioritisation.

skillGaps.skill: Incident response processes and ticket-driven security operations
skillGaps.learningPath: Study NIST incident response guidance and run 3 tabletop scenarios covering phishing, malware, and credential compromise.

skillGaps.skill: Identity and access management including Entra ID, MFA, conditional access, and privileged access
skillGaps.learningPath: Complete Entra ID administrator labs and configure MFA, conditional access, and role-based access controls in a test tenant.

skillGaps.skill: Scripting for security automation with Python or PowerShell
skillGaps.learningPath: Take a beginner Python or PowerShell automation course and build 3 scripts for log parsing, IOC matching, and user audit checks.

skillGaps.skill: Cloud security monitoring in Azure including Defender for Cloud
skillGaps.learningPath: Use Azure trial resources to configure Defender for Cloud recommendations and document secure baseline controls for 2 workloads.
Practice writing weekly one-page security summaries from lab incidents and present them to a peer or mentor for feedback.

skillGaps.skill: Cross-functional collaboration with IT operations, service desk, and compliance teams
skillGaps.learningPath: Join a cross-team project or simulation and document responsibilities, escalation paths, and communication checkpoints.

skillGaps.skill: Prioritisation under pressure during live incidents
skillGaps.learningPath: Use incident simulation exercises with timed triage decisions and review outcomes against severity and business impact.

skillGaps.skill: UK cyber regulatory awareness including UK GDPR, Data Protection Act 2018, and NIS principles
skillGaps.learningPath: Study ICO guidance and NCSC materials, then map 3 common security incidents to UK reporting and data handling obligations.

skillGaps.skill: SOC operating model knowledge including alert triage, escalation, SLAs, and evidence handling
skillGaps.learningPath: Review SOC analyst workflows from Microsoft and Splunk resources and create a sample triage-to-escalation playbook.

aiSkills.description: Understand how supervised and unsupervised models support anomaly detection, phishing detection, and alert prioritisation in security operations.
aiSkills.learningPath: Complete an introductory ML for cybersecurity course and summarise 3 use cases where models improve SOC detection quality.

aiSkills.skill: LLM prompt engineering for security investigations
aiSkills.description: Use structured prompts to summarise alerts, generate incident timelines, and draft investigation notes safely.
aiSkills.learningPath: Practice prompt templates in Azure OpenAI or ChatGPT using sample security logs and compare outputs for accuracy and completeness.

aiSkills.skill: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for security knowledge search
aiSkills.description: Apply RAG patterns to query internal playbooks, threat intel, and policy documents during investigations.
aiSkills.learningPath: Build a simple RAG prototype with a vector database and a small set of security playbooks using Azure AI Search or an open-source stack.

aiSkills.skill: Azure OpenAI and Microsoft Security Copilot awareness
aiSkills.description: Understand Microsoft AI security tooling used for analyst productivity, investigation support, and summarisation.
aiSkills.learningPath: Complete Microsoft Learn modules on Azure OpenAI and Security Copilot and test 5 analyst workflows in a sandbox or demo environment.

aiSkills.skill: Vector databases and embeddings for threat intelligence retrieval
aiSkills.description: Know how embeddings and vector search improve retrieval of indicators, procedures, and prior incidents.
aiSkills.learningPath: Follow a hands-on tutorial using FAISS, Pinecone, or Azure AI Search to index threat reports and query them with semantic search.

aiSkills.description: Apply controls for privacy, hallucination risk, prompt injection, data leakage, and human review in AI-assisted security workflows.
aiSkills.learningPath: Study the NCSC and Microsoft responsible AI guidance and create a one-page control checklist for secure AI use in a SOC.

competencies.competency: Structured incident triage and evidence-based decision making
competencies.description: Structured incident triage and evidence-based decision making

competencies.competency: Risk-based prioritisation aligned to business impact
competencies.description: Risk-based prioritisation aligned to business impact

competencies.competency: Security documentation discipline and chain-of-custody awareness
competencies.description: Security documentation discipline and chain-of-custody awareness

competencies.competency: Operational ownership of recurring security tasks and SLAs
competencies.description: Operational ownership of recurring security tasks and SLAs

strengths: Directly relevant networking and cyber security training reference
Knowledge of common attack types including DoS, DDoS, XSS, password attacks, and SQL attacks
Understanding of encryption, hashing, and networking security concepts
Windows, Linux, and Azure fundamentals exposure
Long overall IT experience and customer-facing support capability
gaps: No explicit job title showing hands-on cyber security operations experience

No scripting or automation evidence for security tasks

recommendations: Add a targeted cyber security summary with SOC, incident response, and security monitoring language
Include hands-on lab projects using Sentinel, Defender, vulnerability scanning, and IAM controls

Reframe networking and support experience to highlight security responsibilities and escalation handling

marketIntelligence.demandGrowth: Strong and still rising across the UK.
marketIntelligence.demandDrivers: More cyber attacks on businesses and public services, More cloud adoption, More regulation and audit pressure, Ongoing shortage of cyber talent
marketIntelligence.medianSalary: About ВЈ38,000-ВЈ45,000 for technician and junior analyst level roles.
marketIntelligence.salaryRange: Roughly ВЈ30,000-ВЈ55,000 depending on location, clearance, and tool experience.
marketIntelligence.contractRates: Often around ВЈ200-ВЈ350 per day for junior to mid-level contract work.
marketIntelligence.remoteDemand: Moderate to high, but many employers prefer hybrid for security operations roles.
marketIntelligence.automationInsight: Routine alert handling is becoming more automated, but human investigation, escalation, and business judgement are still needed.
hiringSignals: High volume of SOC, security operations, and cloud security support vacancies

industryMomentum: Positive across finance, defence, consulting, telecoms, healthcare, and government.
marketIntelligence.futureOutlook: Very positive over the next 3-5 years, especially in cloud security and security operations.
